message:
chore(node): update beads issue tracker with migration fix and verifier toolkit entries

Track bd-22ymr (migration compile regressions) and bd-2u0rd (non-finite
verifier toolkit safety) as closed with verification evidence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-22e7","title":"[5] Method Stack Compliance — 4 mandatory execution disciplines","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md — Section 5

## Why This Exists
franken_node is driven by four complementary methodologies. These are not optional preferences; they are mandatory execution disciplines. Every implementation bead must comply with at least one of these method stacks, and cross-cutting beads must demonstrate compliance with all applicable stacks.

## Four Required Method Stacks

### 5.1 extreme-software-optimization (Execution Discipline)
Mandatory loop for every performance-sensitive change:
1. Baseline (p50/p95/p99, throughput, memory, cold start)
2. Profile (top hotspots only)
3. Prove behavior invariance and compatibility envelopes
4. Implement one lever
5. Verify compatibility/security artifacts
6. Re-profile
NO optimization lands without artifact-backed regression safety.

### 5.2 alien-artifact-coding (Mathematical Decision Core)
Use formal decision systems for product control surfaces:
- Expected-loss rollout choices
- Posterior trust state updates
- Confidence-aware migration recommendations
- Explainable policy decisions and receipts

### 5.3 alien-graveyard (High-EV Primitive Selection)
Adopt only high-EV disruptive primitives with fallback contracts:
- EV thresholding (EV >= 2.0)
- Failure-mode predesign
- Deterministic degraded operation pathways

### 5.4 porting-to-rust (Spec-First Essence Extraction Protocol)
For compatibility surfaces, apply the porting discipline as extraction-and-proof:
- Extract behavior into explicit specs (data shapes, invariants, defaults, errors, edge cases)
- Capture Node/Bun fixture outputs as conformance baselines
- Implement from spec and fixture contracts, NOT legacy source structure
- Enforce parity and divergence visibility via lockstep oracle + artifact gates
RULE: Legacy code is input to specification and oracle generation, NOT implementation blueprint.

## Compliance Mapping to Execution Tracks
- 5.1 (perf discipline): Required for 10.6, 10.14, 10.15, 10.17, 10.18
- 5.2 (math decision): Required for 10.5, 10.17, 10.19, 10.20, 10.21
- 5.3 (high-EV primitives): Required for all 10.x tracks adopting advanced primitives
- 5.4 (spec-first extraction): Required for 10.2, 10.3, 10.7

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each implementation PR cites which method stack(s) it follows
- Performance PRs include before/after baseline artifacts (5.1)
- Decision-surface PRs include formal decision rationale (5.2)
- New primitive adoption PRs include EV analysis and fallback contract (5.3)
- Compatibility PRs include spec reference and fixture IDs (5.4)

## Testing Requirements
- CI gate checking for method stack citation in PR descriptions
- Audit script validating artifact presence for performance changes
- Review checklist enforcing method stack compliance


## Additional Verification Requirements
- Unit tests for method-stack compliance validators (citation checks, artifact-rule checks, stack-to-change classification).
- E2E compliance scripts that execute representative change flows and verify required method-stack evidence is produced end-to-end.
- Structured logs for compliance evaluations with deterministic rule IDs, pass/fail status, and remediation guidance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T16:14:34.097919725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T21:02:44.888350975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T21:02:44.888326650Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting","methodology","plan","section-5"]}
 {"id":"bd-22lo","title":"CRDT conformance fixtures: update OR-Set tests for replica-aware add API","description":"Fresh-eyes audit on recent connector changes found that the OR-Set API in crates/franken-node/src/connector/crdt.rs now requires add(replica_id, element) after the causal-tag refactor, but tests/conformance/crdt_merge_fixtures.rs still calls the old single-argument form. That leaves the Rust conformance fixture surface out of sync with the implementation and can break all-target verification. Update the conformance fixtures to supply deterministic replica IDs while preserving commutativity, associativity, and idempotency intent, then validate with rch-targeted cargo checks/tests only.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AzureCave","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:00:00.104768953Z","created_by":"AzureCave","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:06:48.904047891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T00:06:48.904021972Z","close_reason":"Updated tests/conformance/crdt_merge_fixtures.rs for replica-aware OrSet::add API; checker validation passed and attempted rch cargo target check confirmed this fixture is not a standalone Cargo test target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-22lv8","title":"supply_chain/category_shift.rs: format_iso_timestamp() cast secs:u64 to i64 via 'secs as i64'. For u64 values above i64::MAX, this wraps to a negative number, producing an incorrect timestamp. Fixed by using i64::try_from(secs).ok().and_then() to safely handle the conversion.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-19T17:55:06.770993595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:55:52.171722686Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T17:55:52.171700325Z","close_reason":"Fixed: i64::try_from for safe u64-to-i64 conversion","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Verification: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check exit 0; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/.rch-target-gpt5-v2 cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib exit 0; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/.rch-target-gpt5-v2 cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets exit 0; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/.rch-target-gpt5-v2 cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -- -D warnings exit 0.  {"id":"bd-22yy","title":"[10.14] Add DPOR-style schedule exploration gates for control/epoch/remote protocols.","description":"## Why This Exists

Dynamic Partial Order Reduction (DPOR) is a state-space exploration technique that systematically discovers concurrency bugs by exploring different interleavings of concurrent operations, but pruning equivalent schedules to keep the search tractable. For the 9J runtime, where epoch transitions, remote effects, and marker operations can interleave in complex ways, DPOR exploration is the most rigorous way to verify that no schedule produces a safety violation. Manual testing or random fuzzing cannot provide the coverage guarantees that DPOR offers. This bead directly enforces runtime invariant #9 (deterministic verification gates: protocol correctness is explored, not assumed) and feeds into the conformance suite (bd-3i6c). It targets specific protocol classes: epoch barrier coordination, remote capability operations, and marker stream mutations.

## What This Must Do

1. Implement a DPOR-style schedule explorer in `tests/lab/dpor_protocol_exploration.rs` that takes a protocol model (a set of concurrent operations with dependency annotations) and explores all non-equivalent schedules.
2. Define protocol models for: (a) epoch barrier coordination (propose/drain/commit across N participants), (b) remote capability operations (acquire/execute/release with concurrent epoch transitions), (c) marker stream mutations (concurrent append attempts with fencing).
3. For each model, define safety properties as assertions: (a) no two epochs are active simultaneously, (b) no remote operation executes without valid capability, (c) marker sequence is dense and hash-chain-valid after all operations complete.
4. On safety property violation, emit a minimal counterexample trace: the shortest schedule that triggers the violation, with exact operation ordering and intermediate states.
5. Implement bounded CI budget: the explorer must complete within a configurable time limit (default: 60 seconds per model) and report coverage metrics (explored schedules / estimated total schedules).
6. Produce a scope document defining which protocol classes are covered, what safety properties are checked, and what the coverage targets are.

## Acceptance Criteria

- DPOR explorer covers targeted protocol classes; minimal counterexample traces are emitted on failure; gate runs within bounded CI budget.
- Explorer completes epoch barrier model exploration within 60 seconds for N=3 participants.
- Explorer completes remote capability model exploration within 60 seconds.
- Explorer completes marker stream model exploration within 60 seconds.
- A deliberately buggy model (e.g., barrier that commits without full drain) produces a counterexample trace within 10 seconds.
- Counterexample trace includes exact operation ordering (step-by-step), intermediate states, and the violated safety property.
- Coverage report shows percentage of explored schedules and is included in verification evidence.
- Explorer does not exceed memory budget (configurable, default 1GB).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: exploration of a trivial 2-operation model (verify all interleavings explored); counterexample emission for known-buggy model; timeout enforcement; coverage metric accuracy.
- Integration tests: full exploration of epoch barrier model with 3 participants; full exploration of remote capability model; full exploration of marker stream model; deliberately buggy versions of each model produce counterexamples.
- Conformance tests: `tests/lab/dpor_protocol_exploration.rs` -- the DPOR gate tests.
- Structured logs: `DPOR_EXPLORATION_START` (model_name, estimated_schedules, budget_sec, trace_id), `DPOR_SCHEDULE_EXPLORED` (model_name, schedule_index, trace_id -- debug level), `DPOR_VIOLATION_FOUND` (model_name, property_name, counterexample_length, trace_id), `DPOR_EXPLORATION_COMPLETE` (model_name, explored_count, total_estimated, elapsed_sec, trace_id), `DPOR_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` (model_name, explored_count, budget_sec, trace_id).

## Expected Artifacts

- `tests/lab/dpor_protocol_exploration.rs` -- DPOR explorer and protocol model tests
- `docs/testing/dpor_gate_scope.md` -- scope document defining covered protocols and properties
- `artifacts/10.14/dpor_exploration_summary.json` -- exploration summary with coverage metrics
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-22yy/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-22yy/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

- Upstream: bd-3hdv (monotonic control epoch -- epoch model depends on epoch semantics), bd-ac83 (named remote computation registry -- remote model depends on computation identifiers).
- Downstream: bd-3i6c (conformance suite -- uses DPOR results as evidence), bd-25oa (10.15 canonical DPOR gate), bd-3epz (section gate), bd-5rh (section gate).","acceptance_criteria":"DPOR explorer covers targeted protocol classes; minimal counterexample traces are emitted on failure; gate runs within bounded CI budget.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:59.312014395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:40:51.983527670Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:40:51.983470724Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22yy","depends_on_id":"bd-3hdv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22yy","depends_on_id":"bd-ac83","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2tua","title":"[10.16] Implement `frankensqlite` adapter layer for required `franken_node` persistence surfaces.","description":"## Why This Exists

Once the frankensqlite persistence contract (bd-1a1j) defines which state domains need persistence and at what durability tier, this bead implements the actual adapter layer that routes all franken_node persistence APIs through frankensqlite. This is the core implementation bead for the persistence plane — it replaces any interim/ad-hoc file-based or in-memory stores with a unified frankensqlite-backed persistence surface.

In the three-kernel architecture, franken_node's operational state (fencing tokens, lease state, rollout state, audit logs, CRDT merge state, quarantine records, retention policies, artifact persistence) must survive crashes and support deterministic replay. The adapter layer is the single integration point between franken_node's domain logic and frankensqlite's storage engine, enforcing the durability modes and safety tiers specified in the persistence contract.

## What This Must Do

1. Implement `crates/franken-node/src/storage/frankensqlite_adapter.rs` (new module) containing:
   - A `FrankensqliteAdapter` struct that wraps frankensqlite's connection pool.
   - Trait implementations for each persistence class defined in bd-1a1j's contract:
     - `ControlStatePersistence` — crash-safe writes for fencing tokens, lease state, rollout state (Tier 1, WAL-mode).
     - `AuditLogPersistence` — append-only audit log with replay support (Tier 1).
     - `SnapshotPersistence` — periodic flush for snapshot state, CRDT merge state (Tier 2).
     - `CachePersistence` — best-effort ephemeral cache (Tier 3, memory-mode).
   - Configuration loading from `src/config.rs` for database path, pool size, and durability mode per tier.
   - Schema initialization and migration hooks that delegate to `src/connector/schema_migration.rs`.
   - Structured error types mapping frankensqlite errors to franken_node error codes (from `src/connector/error_code_registry.rs`).

2. Create `tests/integration/frankensqlite_adapter_conformance.rs` containing:
   - Conformance tests for each persistence trait:
     - Write-read round-trip for each tier.
     - Crash simulation (kill adapter mid-write) verifying Tier 1 data survives.
     - Concurrent access tests (multiple tasks reading/writing simultaneously).
     - Replay determinism (write sequence, replay from WAL, verify identical state).
   - Schema migration conformance (apply migration, verify schema, rollback, re-apply).

3. Generate `artifacts/10.16/frankensqlite_adapter_report.json` containing:
   - `conformance_results[]` array with `{test_name, persistence_class, tier, status, latency_ms, notes}`.
   - `coverage_summary` with counts by tier and trait.
   - `schema_migration_status` with `{migrations_applied, rollback_tested, current_version}`.

4. Create verification script `scripts/check_frankensqlite_adapter.py` with `--json` flag and `self_test()`:
   - Validates that every persistence class in the contract has a corresponding adapter implementation.
   - Checks that conformance tests exist for every trait.
   - Verifies the adapter report JSON is complete and has zero failures.

5. Create `tests/test_check_frankensqlite_adapter.py` with unit tests.

6. Produce evidence artifacts:
   - `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-2tua/verification_evidence.json`
   - `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-2tua/verification_summary.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Required persistence APIs route through adapter; conformance tests validate deterministic read/write/replay semantics.
- Every persistence class from the bd-1a1j contract has a corresponding trait implementation in the adapter.
- Tier 1 (crash-safe) classes pass crash simulation tests.
- Concurrent access tests pass without data corruption or deadlocks.
- Replay determinism is verified for all Tier 1 and Tier 2 classes.
- Schema migration is integrated with `src/connector/schema_migration.rs`.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- **Unit tests**: Validate adapter configuration loading, error mapping, and trait method contracts.
- **Integration tests**: Full round-trip persistence cycle for each tier; crash simulation; concurrent access; replay verification.
- **Event codes**: `FRANKENSQLITE_ADAPTER_INIT` (info), `FRANKENSQLITE_WRITE_SUCCESS` (debug), `FRANKENSQLITE_WRITE_FAIL` (error), `FRANKENSQLITE_CRASH_RECOVERY` (warning), `FRANKENSQLITE_REPLAY_START` (info), `FRANKENSQLITE_REPLAY_MISMATCH` (error).
- **Trace correlation**: Transaction ID and persistence class name in all adapter events.
- **Deterministic replay**: All conformance tests use fixed seeds and deterministic data generators; tempfile-backed databases with `tempfile = "3.15"`.

## Expected Artifacts

- `crates/franken-node/src/storage/frankensqlite_adapter.rs`
- `tests/integration/frankensqlite_adapter_conformance.rs`
- `artifacts/10.16/frankensqlite_adapter_report.json`
- `scripts/check_frankensqlite_adapter.py`
- `tests/test_check_frankensqlite_adapter.py`
- `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-2tua/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_16/bd-2tua/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies

- **bd-1a1j** (blocks): The persistence contract must define persistence classes and tiers before the adapter can be built.

## Dependents

- **bd-26ux**: Migration from interim stores depends on the adapter existing.
- **bd-10g0**: Section gate depends on this bead.","acceptance_criteria":"- Required persistence APIs route through adapter; conformance tests validate deterministic read/write/replay semantics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:02.018964620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T21:13:56.243345987Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T21:13:56.243309188Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-16","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tua","depends_on_id":"bd-1a1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2twu","title":"[PROGRAM] Enforce canonical evidence-artifact namespace + collision gate","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md (Cross-cutting verification discipline across Sections 10.0–10.21 and 11–16)
Section: PROGRAM (Cross-cutting verification discipline)

Task Objective:
Define and enforce a canonical evidence-artifact namespace contract so every bead produces deterministic, non-colliding, machine-indexable artifacts that can be replayed and audited without ambiguous filenames or overwritten outputs.

Why This Exists:
Current bead descriptions intentionally preserve capability scope, but many still use generic artifact placeholders. Without explicit namespace rules and automated collision checks, downstream verification can become ambiguous, especially when multiple section gates run concurrently.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Publish canonical artifact naming schema covering unit, integration, e2e, benchmark, and verification outputs.
- Define mandatory metadata fields for artifact manifests (bead id, section, scenario id, seed, profile, timestamp, commit, trace id).
- Add automated collision detector that fails when two beads map to the same canonical artifact path.
- Require all section/program verification gates to consume the canonical manifest and validate completeness.
- Document migration rules for legacy/generic artifact placeholders to canonical paths.

Expected Artifacts:
- docs/verification/ARTIFACT_NAMESPACE_CONTRACT.md
- schemas/artifact_manifest.schema.json
- scripts/verify_artifact_namespace.sh
- artifacts/program/artifact_namespace_validation_report.json

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for schema validator and path canonicalization logic.
- E2E tests that execute multi-section verification workflows and assert collision-free artifact emission.
- Structured logs with stable event codes for namespace resolution, collision checks, and manifest validation outcomes.
- CPU-intensive checks (full matrix/e2e sweeps) must run via rch offload and include worker metadata in logs.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- Preserve full feature scope by strengthening evidence rigor only; no capability reduction or gate relaxation is allowed.
- This bead is additive and must not weaken any existing section-level testing/e2e/logging obligations.
- Outputs must be deterministic and independently replayable without hidden local context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- Prevents false-green verification caused by artifact path collisions or ambiguous outputs.
- Improves incident forensics by making every result traceable to an exact bead/scenario/seed/profile.
- Reduces operator confusion and accelerates root-cause analysis in large-scale, parallel execution flows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:20:37.459103037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T08:37:35.823839955Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T08:37:35.823749517Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","program-integration","test-obligations","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2twu","depends_on_id":"bd-1dpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:04Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Added ct_eq verification of compilation_digest in publish_batch() before scoreboard commit. 36 claim_compiler tests pass including 3 new regression tests (tampered text, tampered evidence, clean claim). rch build exit=0.","created_at":"2026-03-13T08:16:36Z"}]}
